description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a prominent array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust structural base. The solar panels are arranged in a single, slightly elevated row, supported by a metallic frame that appears to be anchored to a flat platform. The base structure is composed of a combination of metal and concrete, providing stability and durability for outdoor urban environments. The panels themselves are dark, likely made of tempered glass and silicon cells, and are tilted to optimize sunlight capture. There are additional components visible beneath the panels, possibly housing electrical connections or control units. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed framework and a compact footprint suitable for placement in public spaces, parking lots, or building rooftops.
description: A compact solar energy installation with a single row of tilted photovoltaic panels on a sturdy metal and concrete base, designed for urban outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full width of the solar panel array and the supporting base structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the tilt angle of the panels and the depth of the supporting frame.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back of the panel array and additional structural or electrical components beneath.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the panel tilt and the mounting structure's profile.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted, supported by a metallic (likely aluminum or steel) frame anchored to a flat base
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 6 panels
